title: Reorganize Branding Page into Color Mode and Branding Sections
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the branding settings page to be split into a free Color Mode section and a premium Branding section, so that I can easily toggle light/dark mode while understanding that custom branding is a premium feature.
description:
Restructure the branding settings page into two distinct sections to separate free and premium features, and to improve the preview experience.

## Layout

### Color Mode (top section)
- Light / Dark mode toggle
- Standard preset color options
- Changes in this section are free and saveable by all users
- If they change branding-specific features without permission, changes are ignored

### Branding (bottom section, premium)
- Custom color picker moved here as the first option
- Tab title customization
- Favicon upload
- Logo upload
- Separate save button that triggers an upgrade prompt for non-premium users (links to MBT-690 upgrade window)

## Premium Gating
- Branding save button triggers the upgrade window popout for users with fewer than 2 active users (free tier)
- Upgrade window content managed via WordPress custom post type `upgrade_windows` with slug "branding"
acceptance_criteria: Branding page is split into Color Mode and Branding sections
Color Mode section shows light/dark toggle and preset colors
Branding section shows custom color, tab title, favicon, and logo options
Free-tier users can save Color Mode changes
Branding section save triggers upgrade window for free-tier users (active users < 2)
Upgrade window content is sourced from WordPress custom post type
Premium users can save all branding changes
ux_changes: Branding page reorganized into two sections: Color Mode (free, at top) and Branding (premium, at bottom). Premium features gated with an upgrade window popup for free-tier users.
middleware_changes: Conditional logic to check active user count and gate branding save behind premium tier. Upgrade window content fetched from WordPress custom post type.
implementation_notes: Page split into two sections with separate save behaviors. Free-tier gating uses active user count (< 2). Upgrade window popout uses a WordPress custom post type called upgrade_windows, fetched by page title/slug 'branding'.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the layout split and added the active users < 2 condition for the premium gate. Used WordPress custom post type title for the upgrade window slug. Product owner reviewed the updated layout.
